turns.user_message: They just walked up to me and handed over another heavy stack of demands, telling me to handle it all by the weekend. I used to just nod and dive right in without a second thought. Now I just stand there feeling this heavy weight settle deep in my chest. I keep telling myself it is just another routine request, but my brain completely refuses to engage. Everything feels so much heavier today than it really should.

They just walked up to me and handed over another heavy stack of demands, telling me to handle it all by the weekend. I used to just nod and dive right in without a second thought, but lately I feel like I am completely running on empty. Every single time they drop something new on me, I notice this dull ache behind my eyes that just will not fade. I try to push through the mental fog, but my focus keeps slipping away no matter how hard I concentrate. It is exhausting to pretend I am still keeping up when I am barely holding on.

They just walked up to me and handed over another heavy stack of demands, telling me to handle it all by the weekend. I used to just nod and dive right in without a second thought, but now the mere sound of their footsteps makes my whole body instantly tense up. I sit here staring at the same blank space for hours while my mind goes completely empty and my hands just hang uselessly in my lap. The pressure builds until I feel like I am drowning in plain sight, unable to process a single word. I just want to close my eyes and let the whole thing disappear, but I know it will still be waiting for me tomorrow.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with something someone else said or did. This used to not bother the speaker.
